key_insight: An unsupervised domain adaptation framework with gradual source-domain expansion enables weld penetration status prediction to transfer robustly between fundamentally different welding processes, including TIG and laser welding.
engineering_relevance: This reduces the need for costly relabeling when deploying intelligent weld monitoring across new processes, improving practical portability of inspection models in manufacturing.
method: Train a UDA model for penetration-state classification across source and target welding domains.
Use gradual source domain expansion (GSDE) to progressively widen source-domain coverage during adaptation.
Validate learned domain-invariant features with UMAP feature visualization.
evidence_signal: The method achieved 90.65% and 90.72% average accuracy in same-process TIG and laser tasks, and 80.48% (TIG→Laser) plus 81.13% (Laser→TIG) in cross-process transfer, substantially outperforming the supervised baseline.
watch_item: Cross-process deployment still depends on representative target-process data characteristics, so performance may drop if welding settings, sensors, or material conditions differ beyond the studied domains.
paper_title: A cross-process welding penetration status prediction algorithm based on unsupervised domain adaptation in laser and TIG welding
